TELL THRILLING TALES

pages.raw_text:
got in on the ground floor and some
of them became rich, but that is an
other story.


what ! was driving at is that
you now see the sequel of the Col
orado deportations out in the Ne
vada fields. A committee of the
miners meets every train and looks
over the passengers. Men who took
part in running out our fellows and
mine owners who were particularly
active in ordering that sort of thing
done, are told that they will find
very good accommodation on the
next train leaving town. Mine own
ers who have earned places on the
list have therefore not found 1t
worth while to make investments
out there.


Those who thought it was such
fine sport to run our men out OF
Cripple Creek do not find it worth
while to stay. If they do not go by
train they are led to the edge of
town and told to travel, just as our
men were led out in Colorado.


IL was told OF one case that
might come under the head of
poetic justice. A group of thugs
that had done duty at Cripple
Creek had been given the word to
git at Goldfeld, when one of the
deporters called for short delay.
He went into a grocery across the
street and came back with a can
that he threw to one of those about
to go.


There he said, are your damn
ed beans, Probably you will remem
Der that when you gave me the
word at Cripple Creek you threw q
can Of beans after EN- and said Il
was good and plenty to last until
got out OF the country. Now 20,
try it,"
